--- Dataset Description ---
  - Name: Resting state with closed eyes for patients with depression and healthy participants
  - BIDS Version: 1.0.2

--- Cohort Size ---
  - Num...

modifications.rationale: Bias field correction improves intensity homogeneity, especially important for thick-slice T1 images with variable voxel sizes to enhance downstream segmentation and registration.
FSL-BET with robustfov better handles anisotropic, thick-slice data than the default BET, yielding more accurate brain masks for this dataset.
Enabling robustfov improves brain extraction robustness in the presence of slice thickness and partial volume effects typical of this cohort.
The dataset uses a closed-eyes resting state; removing eye tissue reduces contamination in the brain mask and downstream analyses.
ACPC alignment standardizes head orientation across subjects, facilitating more reliable cross-subject comparisons for connectivity analyses.

Discarding the first 5 TRs (≈12.5 s) is standard practice to allow magnetization to reach steady state and reduce transient motion artifacts, improving data quality for resting‑state connectivity analyses.
The dataset lacks fieldmaps or reverse phase‑encoded pairs required for distortion correction; running the module would produce errors or unreliable results, so it is safer to disable it.
Preserve higher anatomical detail for accurate cortical parcellation and connectivity estimation; dataset likely contains 1mm T1-weighted images.
Improved interpolation quality for coregistering thick-slice functional data to high-resolution anatomy, reducing interpolation artifacts.
Cross-correlation is more robust to intensity inhomogeneities across subjects for T1 registration, often yielding better alignment than mutual information.
Enable ICA-AROMA to automatically identify and regress out motion-related components, improving the quality of functional connectivity estimates for classification.
Resample nuisance regressors to template space to ensure consistent alignment across subjects, facilitating group-level connectivity analysis.
